heading: Training to operate certain aircraft
label: 44939 Training to operate certain aircraft
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (d). substituted “The Secretary of Homeland Security” for “Not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary” in introductory provisions.Pub. (a) to (d) relating to waiting period for training, interruption of training, covered training, and security awareness training for employees.Pub. L. 108–176

Flight Instructor Certificates, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–63, title VIII, § 820138 Stat. 1330May 16, 2024“Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Administrator [of the Federal Aviation Administration] shall issue a final rule for the rulemaking activity titled ‘Removal of the Expiration Date on a Flight Instructor Certificate’, published in Fall 2022 in the Unified Agenda of Federal Regulatory and Deregulatory Actions (RIN 2120–AL25) to, at a minimum, update part 61 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, to—May 16, 2024“(1)remove the expiration date on a flight instructor certificate; and“(2)replace the requirement that a flight instructor renews their flight instructor certificate with appropriate recent experience requirements for the holder of a flight instructor certificate to exercise the privileges of such certificate.”

Implementation, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–176, title VI, § 612(b)117 Stat. 2574Dec. 12, 2003“(1).—In generalNot later than 60 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of Homeland Security shall promulgate an interim final rule to implement , United States Code, as amended by subsection (a).Dec. 12, 2003section 44939 of title 49“(2).—Use of overseas facilitiesIn order to implement , United States Code, as amended by subsection (a), United States Embassies and Consulates that possess appropriate fingerprint collection equipment and personnel certified to capture fingerprints shall provide fingerprint services to aliens covered by that section if the Secretary requires fingerprints in the administration of that section, and shall transmit the fingerprints to the Secretary or other agency designated by the Secretary. The Attorney General and the Secretary of State shall cooperate with the Secretary of Homeland Security in carrying out this paragraph.section 44939 of title 49“(3).—Use of united states facilitiesIf the Secretary of Homeland Security requires fingerprinting in the administration of , United States Code, the Secretary may designate locations within the United States that will provide fingerprinting services to individuals covered by that section.”section 44939 of title 49

Report, , , provided that, not later than 1 year after , the Secretary of Homeland Security would submit to Congress a report on the effectiveness of the activities carried out under this section in reducing risks to aviation and national security.Pub. L. 108–176, title VI, § 612(d)117 Stat. 2574Dec. 12, 2003Dec. 12, 2003

L. 107–71, title I, § 113(c)115 Stat. 622Nov. 19, 2001“The Secretary of Transportation, in consultation with the Secretary of State, shall work with the International Civil Aviation Organization and the civil aviation authorities of other countries to improve international aviation security through screening programs for flight instruction candidates.”

children.label: A
children.text: full name, including any aliases used by the applicant or variations in spelling of the applicant’s name;

children.label: B
children.text: passport and visa information;

children.label: C
children.text: country of citizenship;

children.label: D
children.text: date of birth;

children.label: E
children.text: dates of training; and

children.label: F
children.text: fingerprints collected by, or under the supervision of, a Federal, State, or local law enforcement agency or by another entity approved by the Federal Bureau of Investigation or the Secretary of Homeland Security, including fingerprints taken by United States Government personnel at a United States embassy or consulate; and

the Secretary has not directed, within 30 days after being notified under paragraph (1), that person not to provide the requested training because the Secretary has determined that the individual presents a risk to aviation or national security.

children.heading: .—Interruption of Training
children.label: b .—Interruption of Training
children.text: If the Secretary of Homeland Security, more than 30 days after receiving notification under subsection (a) from a person providing training described in subsection (a), determines that the individual presents a risk to aviation or national security, the Secretary shall immediately notify the person providing the training of the determination and that person shall immediately terminate the training.

children.heading: .—Notification
children.label: c .—Notification
children.text: A person operating as a flight instructor, pilot school, or aviation training center or subject to regulation under this part may provide training in the operation of any aircraft having a maximum certificated takeoff weight of 12,500 pounds or less to an alien (as defined in section 101(a)(3) of the Immigration and Nationality Act ()) or to any other individual specified by the Secretary of Homeland Security only if that person has notified the Secretary that the individual has requested such training and furnished the Secretary with that individual’s identification in such form as the Secretary may require.8 U.S.C. 1101(a)(3)

holds an airman’s certification of a foreign country that is recognized by an agency of the United States, including a military agency, that permits an individual to operate a multi-engine aircraft that has a certificated takeoff weight of more than 12,500 pounds;

is employed by a foreign air carrier that is certified under part 129 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, and that has a security program approved under , Code of Federal Regulations;section 1546 of title 49
is an individual that has unescorted access to a secured area of an airport designated under section 44936(a)(1)(A)(ii); or

is an individual that is part of a class of individuals that the Secretary has determined that providing aviation training to presents minimal risk to aviation or national security because of the aviation training already possessed by such class of individuals.

children.heading: .—Training
children.label: e .—Training
children.text: In subsection (a), the term “training” means training received from an instructor in an aircraft or aircraft simulator and does not include recurrent training, ground training, or demonstration flights for marketing purposes.

children.heading: .—Nonapplicability to Certain Foreign Military Pilots
children.label: f .—Nonapplicability to Certain Foreign Military Pilots
children.text: The procedures and processes required by subsections (a) through (d) shall not apply to a foreign military pilot endorsed by the Department of Defense for flight training in the United States and seeking training described in subsection (e) in the United States.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security may assess a fee for an investigation under this section, which may not exceed $100 per individual (exclusive of the cost of transmitting fingerprints collected at overseas facilities) during fiscal years 2003 and 2004. For fiscal year 2005 and thereafter, the Secretary may adjust the maximum amount of the fee to reflect the costs of such an investigation.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be credited to the account in the Treasury from which the expenses were incurred and shall be available to the Secretary for those expenses; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—Interagency Cooperation
children.label: h .—Interagency Cooperation
children.text: The Attorney General, the Director of Central Intelligence, and the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration shall cooperate with the Secretary in implementing this section.

children.heading: .—Security Awareness Training for Employees
children.label: i .—Security Awareness Training for Employees
children.text: The Secretary shall require flight schools to conduct a security awareness program for flight school employees to increase their awareness of suspicious circumstances and activities of individuals enrolling in or attending flight school.
